summary: Wired co-build diagnostic flow end-to-end. System prompt already contained full Co-Build Mode section (394 words) with trigger offer, 5-question diagnostic tree, conversational rules (3-5 questions max, one at a time, skip known answers), smooth exit script, and tool references. Handler functions startCoBuild() and submitDiagnostic() already existed with proper logic (in-memory Map keyed by callId, merges partial updates, sets collectedFollowups flag for end-of-call pickup). VAPI tool definitions already registered in assistant config with correct parameter schemas. The missing piece was the tool-calls switch — added startCoBuild and submitDiagnostic cases and updated the available_tools fallback list. Build compiles clean.
what_was_already_done: VAPI tool definitions in assistant config (lines 1918-1970)
Co-Build Mode section in system prompt
startCoBuild and submitDiagnostic listed in system prompt Tools section
what_d2_wired: Updated available_tools in default fallback to include both new tools
learnings: Most of D2's deliverables were already implemented — the only gap was the tool-calls switch wiring. Prior builds or manual work had created the functions, interfaces, VAPI definitions, and system prompt sections but missed connecting them in the dispatch switch.
The submitDiagnostic handler correctly sets collectedFollowups.set(callId, true) which triggers the follow-up flow in end-of-call-report — this is the critical handoff point for D3/D5 to build upon.
coBuildDiagnostics Map is in-memory only — if the service restarts between tool call and end-of-call-report, diagnostic data is lost. Acceptable for MVP but worth noting for D5 integration.
